title: T-075 — rename POSTHOG_KEY → POSTHOG_API_KEY + .env.example (FT-015)
prompt:
# T-075 — rename POSTHOG_KEY → POSTHOG_API_KEY + .env.example (FT-015)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-015** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (XS effort)

## Goal
Replace all occurrences of POSTHOG_KEY with POSTHOG_API_KEY and add a regression test that scans for the old name.

## Implementation contract
1. Grep + replace POSTHOG_KEY → POSTHOG_API_KEY in apps/web and scripts.
2. Document POSTHOG_API_KEY in apps/web/.env.example with a one-line comment.
3. Unit test scans `apps/web/**/*.ts` and `apps/web/**/*.tsx` for `POSTHOG_KEY` (not `POSTHOG_API_KEY`); fails if any match.

## Files (scope)
- apps/web/.env.example [modify]
- apps/web/app/api/flags/route.ts [modify]
- scripts/__tests__/env-naming.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the codebase, when grep runs for `\bPOSTHOG_KEY\b`, then zero matches.
2. Given apps/web/.env.example, when read, then POSTHOG_API_KEY is present with a comment.
3. Given the env-naming regression test, when run, then it passes.

## Test strategy
- RED: write env-naming.test.ts grepping for POSTHOG_KEY; should fail before rename.
- Run `pnpm test env-naming`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not break the existing flags route during rename.
- Do not commit a real PostHog key.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Rename POSTHOG_KEY → POSTHOG_API_KEY across the codebase and document in apps/web/.env.example.
